steps.title: Spot the contrast signal
steps.content: Although, on average, studies of fish showed larger differences than studies of birds did, for every class of animals examined, there were individual studies showing differences well above the average for fish.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D matches the signal because it says some bird studies found larger effects than some fish studies, which reflects the existence of individual bird studies above the fish average.

Choices A, B, and C each add details not supported by the signal—such as specifying detrimental effects, claiming birds show larger differences overall, or comparing a single study to an average.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
